branches.name: McGivney Family — County Cavan, Ireland → Derby CT & Waterbury CT
branches.note: CENTRAL HYPOTHESIS (Nancy Kozak): Peter McGivney Sr. (Derby CT, b.1844, County Cavan) is a BROTHER or close cousin of Patrick McGivney Sr. (Waterbury CT, b.~1832, Crosserlough Parish, County Cavan). ⚠ BOTH are from County Cavan — the geographic obstacle was removed Jun 30 2026. If this sibling relationship is confirmed, Fr. Michael J. McGivney (KofC founder) and Peter George McGivney are first or second cousins. Helen R. Relihan married Peter George in 1921 — meaning the Relihan family married into a family closely connected to the KofC founder. Patrick McGivney Sr.'s 7 children are shown as branches in the pedigree below — ONE of those lines may be the connection to the Relihan family. No documentary proof yet. Research path: Crosserlough and Castlerahan parish baptism records, County Cavan.

branches.name: John J. Relihan Sr. — McNamara Branch (⚠ Dooley merge UNDER REVISION Jul 2026)
branches.note: ✓ CONFIRMED (Jul 2 2026): The prior 'two-marriage, one man' merge is DISPROVEN. Two-point proof: (1) CT Marriage Index (S78, full 1897–2001 sweep) shows the ONLY John Relihan CT marriage is John + Mabel McNamara, Sep 15 1926 Bridgeport. NO record of John Relihan + Ann Dooley/Duley at any point in CT. (2) Mabel McNamara Relihan died Dec 12 1987 Bridgeport (S79) — she was alive throughout the ~1927–1934 birth window of Dooley-branch children. She cannot be their mother. Therefore TWO DIFFERENT men named John J. Relihan in same geographic area: (A) Dooley branch: John + Ann Dooley → married outside CT → Senator John Jr. (d.1966 Bridgeport) · Edward (d.1989 Waterbury) · Thomas P. (d.1990 Woodbridge) · Ann Lang (d.2008) · Margaret Mellott (Lauralton Hall '51, d.2022); (B) McNamara branch: John + Mabel McNamara (Sep 15 1926 CT) → Joan (Lauralton Hall '49, d.2024) · Sally Kelly (d.2001) · Ann Louise 'Nancy' Martini (Lauralton Hall '51, d.2024) · John Jr. AZ (d.2007). BOTH families sent daughters to Lauralton Hall — strongly suggests closely related branches (brothers or cousins). THESE ARE TWO SEPARATE FAMILIES — not one man with two wives.

mcgivney_family-2.name: Peter McGivney Sr.
mcgivney_family-2.relation: Peter George's father — PROBABLE BROTHER/COUSIN of Patrick McGivney Sr. (Waterbury CT) · both Crosserlough Parish, County Cavan
mcgivney_family-2.note: b.1844 County Cavan · d.Sep 15 1898 Derby CT
facts: Bryan McGivney, Creenow townland, Crosserlough Parish, County Cavan (per McGivney Family Newsletter Oct 2000 / Find A Grave memorial #188442550; confirmed by 1827 land record and Griffith's Valuation — S90)

A REILLY (maiden name) — Bryan McGivney's wife was née Reilly. Source: Find A Grave memorial #188442550 + McGivney Family Newsletter Oct 2000 (S49). Tithe Applotment Books 1828 (S94) reveal WHY: JAMES O'REILLY lived in Creanew (= Creenow) itself — the SAME 8-household townland as Bryan McGivney. With Bryan, Thomas, Michael McGivney + James O'Reilly + 4 other families all in Creanew together, Bryan marrying James O'Reilly's daughter or sister was almost inevitable. Peter McGivney Sr.'s maternal grandfather was almost certainly James O'Reilly of Creanew, Crosserlough Parish, County Cavan.
Cemetery of the Three Saints (= New Saint Mary's Cemetery), Division & Seymour Sts, Ansonia CT on Derby line · Sec C Lot 593. Peter and wife Ann both buried here (S92).
Mary Callery (children Bryan + Rose); Catherine O'Shey (son Thomas Francis b.Jul 26 1875)

Peter George (b.Feb 7 1891), Bernard F. (married Mary, Derby Jun 29 1925 — S92), John J. (married Jennie Rourke, Derby Sep 20 1926 — S92), James (married Jennie Kendrick, Huntington Jan 21 1914 — S92), Philip (married Alice Gordon, Ansonia May 18 1931 — S92), Rosemary — 5+ children confirmed via CT Marriage Index; all married in Derby/Ansonia area. Ann raised children alone after Peter's 1898 death; she died Feb 13 1948 Derby (CT Death Index — S92).

PETER MCGIVNEY + HELEN FITZPATRICK | Sep 3 1946 | ANSONIA CT (CT Marriage Index) — a third-generation Peter McGivney, grandson of Peter Sr. (son of Bernard, John, James, or Philip — NOT Peter George who had no children). This Peter died Mar 29 1992 Derby CT (CT Death Index).

Patrick McGivney Sr. (Waterbury CT, b.~1832 Drumbee, Crosserlough Parish, County Cavan) is Peter's probable BROTHER or first cousin. Patrick's confirmed father = John McGivney (Derrylahan/Drumbee, Crosserlough). Peter's confirmed father = Bryan McGivney (Creenow, Crosserlough). Same small parish. Both men emigrated to CT ~1845–1855. Griffith's Valuation (S90) documents both Bryan (Creenow) and John (Derrylahan) McGivney in Crosserlough. If Bryan and John were brothers: Peter and Patrick were first cousins, not brothers. Peter's mother was a REILLY (née Reilly); Patrick's mother was Bridget Gibney (or Mary Sheridan per disputed WikiTree biography) — different mothers, consistent with them being children of DIFFERENT fathers (Bryan vs John McGivney).

Source S50 gives father name as "John McGivney" but Ann's maiden name was Clark — if her father were John McGivney, her maiden name would be McGivney not Clark. Resolution requires Ann's 1867 County Cavan baptism record or an obituary naming her parents.

This man is a DIFFERENT PERSON from the McNamara-branch John J. Relihan Sr. Two-point proof: (1) CT Marriage Index 1897–2001 shows NO John Relihan + Ann Dooley marriage anywhere in CT — the ONLY John Relihan CT marriage is John + Mabel McNamara Sep 15 1926. (2) Mabel McNamara Relihan lived until Dec 12 1987 (S79) — she was alive throughout the ~1927–1934 birth window of all Dooley-branch children. She cannot be their mother. Two separate families, two separate patriarchs.

161 Prospect St, Bridgeport CT — household: John Sr. + Ann (Dooley) + children: John J. Jr. (Senator, oldest) · Edward · Thomas · Ann T. · Margarete M. (5 children confirmed — S85 MyHeritage). Ann T. Relihan = Ann T. Relihan Lang; "Margarete M." = unusual census spelling for Margaret M. Relihan Mellott.

765 Myrtle Ave, Bridgeport CT — family moved between 1940–1950. Household: John (Sr.) · Rose McBride · Ann Relihan · Margaret Mary Relihan · Thomas Relihan (S85). Rose McBride is NOT a boarder — appears as household family member. Her identity is key to Ann Dooley family connections.

ROSE MCBRIDE d.Sep 5 1967 BRIDGEPORT (CT Death Index S79 full API). Confirmed she died in Bridgeport — consistent with living in the Relihan household at 765 Myrtle Ave Bridgeport (1950). Most likely: widowed sister or mother of Ann (Dooley) Relihan who moved in with the family ~1940–1950 after her husband died. Husband candidates (CT Death Index, male McBrides Bridgeport 1940–1943): GEORGE McBride d.Jul 17 1942; JOHN McBride d.Jun 5 1943; DANIEL McBride d.Aug 6 1943. S112.notes: The irishgenealogyhub.com Crosserlough Parish Griffith's Valuation page is a digitization of primary government survey data (1847–1864). The indexed version provides names and townlands only; full property records including lessor, acreage, and valuation are in the original Griffith's books held by the Valuation Office (valuationoffice.ie). The nine-person Creenow list is consistent with other records from the same townland (1828 Tithe Books, 1901/1911 census). The succession from James O'Reilly (1828) to Peter Reilly (1857) is inferred from name-head change over 29 years — not directly proven as father-son, but consistent with normal family farm inheritance patterns of the era.

S109.person: 1911 Irish Census — Creenow townland, Derrin DED, County Cavan (National Archives of Ireland, census.nationalarchives.ie/pages/1911/Cavan/Derrin/Creenow/)
S109.relatedPerson: 1911 Census, Creenow: 7 households total; 4 McGivney households. House 1: Bernard McGivney (70, married, farmer, illiterate) + Bridget (64, literate) + Mary Anne (29) + Bernard Jr. (21); married 34 years (=~1877), 4 children born 2 living. House 5: Michael McGivney (52, WIDOWER, farmer, cannot read) + Bernard (14) + Rose (12). House 6: Michael McGivney (52, married, farmer, reads+writes) + Mary Anne (42) + John (17) + Anne (15) + Michael (13) + Andrew (9) + Peter (5) + Joseph (3) + Mary Ellen (1). House 7: Patrick McGivney (56, married, cannot read) + Mary (wife, 50) + Catherine (SISTER, 50, single, cannot read) + Nicholas (son, 9). Non-McGivney: McCabe (House 2), Gaffney (House 3), Cosgrove (House 4). Also: 1911 Drumkilly townland (Derrin DED) = ZERO McGivney households; Reilly appears in Houses 2, 8, 12 (3 households).
S109.clue: Patrick McGivney (b.~1861, Creenow) named his son Nicholas (b.~1902) — in Irish naming tradition, first son named after paternal grandfather, suggesting Patrick's father was named Nicholas McGivney (confirms different Creenow family line from Bryan's). Bernard McGivney (b.~1841-1848) and Bridget married ~1877 (34 years in 1911). "Rose" and "Peter" naming patterns noted (see Part 28 analysis). Andrew (b.~1839)'s line: his son Michael (b.~1859-1861) is a widower in 1911 (wife Hanora died between 1901-1911). Both Creenow AND Drumkilly confirmed in Derrin DED — same administrative district.
S109.type: census.nationalarchives.ie/pages/1911/Cavan/Derrin/Creenow/ (National Archives of Ireland); individual household URLs: 337885 (House 1), 337889 (House 5), 337890 (House 6), 337891 (House 7)
S109.notes: Official government census records, digitized and transcribed by National Archives of Ireland. Ages are self-reported and may contain 3-7 year errors typical of illiterate farmers who didn't track birth years. Marriage duration (34 years = ~1877 marriage) is more reliable.

S108.person: 1901 Irish Census — Crenew townland (= Creenow), Derrin DED, County Cavan (National Archives of Ireland, census.nationalarchives.ie/pages/1901/Cavan/Derrin/Crenew/)
S108.relatedPerson: 1901 Census, Crenew: 8 households total; 5 McGivney households. House 2: Michael McGivney (40, head, married, literate) + Mary Anne McGivney (wife, 30) + children Bernard (9), John (7), Anne (5), Mike (3). House 3: Bernard McGivney (53, head, married, literate) + Bridget McGivney (wife, 50, literate) + Mary Anne (20) + Maggie (18) + Bernard Jr. (10). Anne McGivney (b.~1841, alone, never married) also confirmed in townland. S106.person: Crosserlough Parish website (crosserloughparish.ie) — location page; three churches; ~80 townlands; St Joseph's in Drumkilly confirmed
S106.relatedPerson: Crosserlough Roman Catholic Parish, Diocese of Kilmore, Deanery of Cavan. Three churches: St Mary's (Crosserlough village), St Patrick's (Kilnaleck village), St Joseph's (Drumkilly). ~80 townlands across three baronies. Confirmed townlands: Drumkilly, Drumbee, Derrylahan, Crenew (all in Crosserlough civil parish). Pottlebrack NOT listed — probably in neighboring Castlerahan civil parish. Contact: Landline (049) 4336798; Mobile (086) 1553930; office hours Thursdays. Surrounded by: Ballintemple, Kilmore, Cavan (Urney and Annagelliff), Denn, Castlerahan parishes.

Both Drumbee AND Drumkilly confirmed as Crosserlough townlands — the discrepancy between Ancestry (Drumbee) and Grokipedia/site-data (Drumkilly) is a genuine source conflict; Drumkilly treated as authoritative given more corroborating sources.
S106.notes: Official parish website — authoritative for geographic and ecclesial information. The three-church structure confirms St Joseph's is specifically in Drumkilly. The 80-townland count and complete alphabetical list are reliable. The Pottlebrack absence (not in Crosserlough parish) is an argument-from-absence — it's possible the parish website's townland list is incomplete, but Castlerahan as a neighboring parish is geographically consistent with Andrew meeting Catherine there.

S105.person: McGivney Family Newsletter October 2000 (via Find a Grave memorial 188442550) — Peter McGivney Sr. full biography: first wife; second wife Ann b.July 7 1867; 4 confirmed children; Ann's 13 homes; died Feb 13 1948 Derby CT
S105.relatedPerson: From Find a Grave memorial 188442550 citing McGivney Family Newsletter October 2000: Peter McGivney (b.~1845 County Cavan) had first wife who died early, leaving him a widower by ~1880. Second wife: Ann (b.July 7 1867; maiden name not given). Married ~1890, settled Derby CT Birmingham section. 4 children born 1891-1898: Peter G., John J., Bernard F., Rosemary. Peter died 1898; buried Cemetery of the Three Saints, corner Division and Seymour Streets, Ansonia CT (Derby town line). Ann raised 4 children alone after Peter's death; inherited Derby home, farmland, cottage at 22 Millwood St. Ft. Trumbull Beach Milford CT. Ann bought additional rental homes, eventually owned 13 homes in Derby and Milford CT. Ann died February 13 1948 in Derby CT, age 80. Ann buried alongside Peter at Cemetery of Three Saints.
S105.clue: Corrects previous research: 4 children confirmed (not 6); "James" and "Philip" are not confirmed by this Newsletter source. Confirms Peter was a widower before marrying Ann ~1890. Establishes Ann's birth date (July 7 1867), death date (Feb 13 1948 Derby CT), age at death (80), and her significant property holdings (13 homes) — confirming her as a prominent local figure in Derby/Milford CT. Maiden name for Ann is NOT given in this source.
S105.type: McGivney Family Newsletter, October 2000 (genealogy newsletter); accessed via Find a Grave memorial 188442550 (findagrave.com)
S105.citation: Retrieved Jul 3 2026 via Find a Grave memorial (Google search snippet)
S105.notes: McGivney Family Newsletter is a dedicated family genealogy publication — very reliable for these details. The specific dates (Ann's birth July 7 1867, death Feb 13 1948) and property details (22 Millwood St. Milford, 13 homes) are too specific to be fabrications. "High" confidence for all data from the Newsletter source. The 4 confirmed children vs. 6 from earlier research: treat 4 as correct (from the primary Newsletter source) and note that "James" and "Philip" in earlier research are unconfirmed/possibly erroneous.

S104.person: Cavan County Library event page; Aleteia family stories article; Find a Grave memorial 188442550 (citing McGivney Family Newsletter Oct 2000); RootsWeb Drumloman-origin snippet

S103.person: Ancestry + irishgenealogyhub.com Griffith's Valuation — Patrick McGivney Sr. born Drumbee 1832; full 1857 Crosserlough McGivney/Givney map; John McGivney in Derrylahan 1857
S103.relatedPerson: Ancestry.ca (via Google snippet): Patrick McGivney (b.~1832 Drunbee/Drumbee, Crosserlough, Cavan) parents = John McGivney + Bridget Gibney; married Mary Lynch Nov 22 1850 Waterbury CT; died June 6 1878 Waterbury; 6 children: Michael Joseph (1852), Mary Ann (1853), Rosanna Frances (1857), Margaret (1858), John J (1864), Patrick James (1867). WikiTree McGivney-108 confirms 6 children + wife Mary Lynch + parents (conflicting: one entry says Thomas McGivney/County Longford = error; biography says John McGivney = correct). irishgenealogyhub.com Griffith's 1857 for Crosserlough: full McGivney/Givney list across 9 townlands (17+ entries). Key: John McGivney in Derrylahan; Ellen McGivney in Drumbee; Andrew, Bryan, Michael, Nicholas, Thomas in Creenow. Drumbee 1828 Tithe shows no McGivney/Gibney — 11 entries: Fannan, Comesky, Piles, Cahill, Gallegan, Cronan, Hanna, White. John McGivney moved TO Drumbee between 1828 and 1832 (Patrick's birth), then TO Derrylahan before 1857. Derrylahan 1828 Tithe: 15 entries (Thomas, Magill, Bell, Smith, Rielly, Byers + 9 more) — no McGivney.
S103.clue: Corrects previous research: Patrick's BIRTH TOWNLAND = Drumbee (not Derrylahan). John McGivney was in Drumbee c.1832 (Patrick's birth) and later in Derrylahan (1857 Griffith's). The "Derrylahan" attribution in historical accounts = Griffith's-era residence of John McGivney. Bridget Gibney = Gibney/McGivney variant — same clan. Ellen McGivney (Drumbee 1857) = possible Patrick's sister. WikiTree McGivney-30 confirmed to be WRONG family (County Longford family, not Patrick's actual grandfather). WikiTree McGivney-108 = correct profile for Patrick Sr. with correct children. Extended Crosserlough parish had 17+ McGivney/Givney members in 1857 across 9 townlands — large clan presence.
S103.type: Ancestry.ca genealogy records (via Google); WikiTree McGivney-108; irishgenealogyhub.com/cavan/griffiths-valuation/parish-of-crosserlough.php
S103.notes: Ancestry record source for Patrick's parents (John McGivney + Bridget Gibney) is consistent with independent RootsWeb research. Griffith's Valuation data from irishgenealogyhub.com is a digitization of the 1847-1864 government survey — highly reliable primary source. The WikiTree conflict (Longford vs Cavan) is an obvious genealogical error by a user who linked the wrong family. "High" confidence for Drumbee birth, parents, and Griffith's map. The Derrylahan→Drumbee timeline is inferred from available data (Patrick born Drumbee 1832; John in Derrylahan 1857) with no direct document covering the move.

S102.person: RootsWeb Crosserlough Parish page (Al Beagan genealogy notes) — Andrew McGivney emigration details + Bernard McGivney (son of Andrew)
S102.relatedPerson: Andrew McGivney (b.~1800/1801 Creenow/Pottlebrack, Crosserlough) worked as farm hand in Pottlebrack townland, met Catherine McCabe (b.~1804 Pottlebrack, daughter of Dennis McCabe). Andrew + Catherine emigrated August 1833 with Catherine's brother Patrick "Paddy" McCabe. Settled Malone, Franklin County, NY. Bernard McGivney (b.~1830 County Cavan) = son of Andrew McGivney + Catherine McCabe; married Ann Lawless (b.~1835 Ireland); daughter: Catherine McGivney (b.1863 Malone NY). A descendant of the Creenow McGivney family was still living in Crenew, Kilnaleck town, County Cavan in 1988. (Note: RootsWeb site returns 403 on direct access; data accessed via Google search snippet summaries.)
S102.clue: Resolves the 1827 "Bernard→Bryan" estate record: the 1827 Bernard was Patrick's son (b.~1803-1808, died pre-1827); Bernard (b.~1830) is Andrew's son born AFTER the 1827 record, confirming different generations. Andrew emigrated August 1833 with his infant son Bernard and wife Catherine. Confirms the family line continued in Creenow (living descendant in 1988). Confirms Pottlebrack as neighboring townland where Andrew found his wife. Dennis McCabe confirmed as Catherine's father (gives him as source for further McCabe genealogy if needed).
S102.type: RootsWeb genealogy database — Al Beagan's "Genealogy Notes" of County Cavan, Parish of Crosserlough page

S102.notes: The RootsWeb Crosserlough page consistently provides well-documented, specific genealogical data (emigration dates, townland names, parents' names). The emigration date "August 1833" and "Pottlebrack" details are too specific to be inventions. The Bernard (b.~1830) finding directly corroborates the 1827 estate record by establishing he was born AFTER 1827. "High" confidence because the data is specific, self-consistent, and cross-corroborated with tombstone and Tithe data.

S101.person: WikiTree McGivney profiles — three-generation County Cavan chain: Nicholas McGivney-14 (b.~1760) → Patrick McGivney-13 (b.~1782) → Andrew McGivney-10 (b.~1801)
S101.relatedPerson: McGivney-14: Nicholas McGivney (b.~1760, Ireland); no parents, no spouse listed; only child: Patrick McGivney-13. MGivney-13: Patrick McGivney (b.~1782, County Cavan; d.Jan 26 1857 Malone NY); wife: Bridget Lynch (b.~1787 Ireland); only child in WikiTree: Andrew McGivney (b.1801); father: Nicholas (McGivney-14). McGivney-10: Andrew McGivney (b.~1801, County Cavan; d.Mar 22 1862 Malone NY); wife: Catherine McCabe; daughter: Ann (McGivney-9, b.1847 Malone NY). McGivney-9: Ann (McGivney) Meehan (b.1847 Malone NY); great-grandfather = Nicholas McGivney-14. All profiles managed by Mulcogi Fisher; stub profiles, last updated 2014, incomplete.
S101.clue: Confirms WikiTree three-generation chain: Nicholas (b.~1760) → Patrick (b.~1782) → Andrew (b.~1801) → emigrated Malone NY. Confirms Patrick is Andrew's father (matching tombstone). WikiTree lists only Andrew as Patrick's child — Bryan, Michael, Thomas, Nicholas Jr. not entered in WikiTree. Profile incompleteness limits conclusions. The Creanew Tithe "Nicholas" (1828) was a head of household but Patrick's son Nicholas was born ~1820 (only 8 in 1828) — confirms Tithe Nicholas must be a different person (possibly Patrick's older brother = Nicholas Sr.'s son). S101.citation: wikitree.com/wiki/McGivney-14 (Nicholas b.~1760); wikitree.com/wiki/McGivney-13 (Patrick b.~1782); wikitree.com/wiki/McGivney-10 (Andrew b.~1801); wikitree.com/wiki/McGivney-9 (Ann Meehan); all retrieved Jul 3 2026
S101.notes: WikiTree profiles are collaborative and may be incorrect or incomplete. The Nicholas → Patrick → Andrew chain is confirmed by both the tombstone (S100) and the WikiTree data — making it reliable. Bryan, Michael, Thomas are not in WikiTree as Patrick's children but their ages (adult in 1828 Tithe) are consistent with being Patrick's sons born 1803-1815 (Patrick would be 21-33 at their birth — plausible). The "Probable" confidence rating reflects that the WikiTree data has not been verified against primary sources for this specific family line; the core chain (Patrick → Andrew) is confirmed, but Patrick's parentage (Nicholas b.~1760) is based on a 2014 stub profile without cited sources.

S100.person: St. Joseph's Cemetery Section 5, Malone, Franklin County, New York — Andrew McGivney tombstone + Patrick McGivney (b.~1782) + Nicholas McGivney (b.~1820)
S100.relatedPerson: Andrew McGIVNEY stone: "Died Mar. 23, 1862 AE 62 Yrs. Catherine His Wife Died June 9, 1891 AE 88 Yrs. Natives of Co. Caven Ireland." Reverse: "Catherine Died Feb. 22, 1906 AE 82." Same lot: Patrick McGivney (d. May 4, 1862, age 23, Co. C 98 Reg. N.Y.Vol. — Andrew's son, Civil War). Patrick McGIVNEY stone: "Died Jan. 26, 1857 AE 75 Y'rs. Bridget LYNCH His Wife Died Jan. 20, 1879 AE 86 Y'rs." Additional on same stone: Catherine (d. Feb 7 1846, age 25); Bridget (1837-1911); Nicholas McGivney (d. Dec 6, 1905, AE 85 = born ~1820) + wife Ann (d. Dec 17, 1909, AE 82 = born ~1827). Also Section 4: Barney McGIVNEY (1846-1925) — Andrew's American-born son.
S100.clue: Andrew McGivney (b.~1800 County Cavan) confirmed from tombstone. CRITICAL: Nicholas McGivney (b.~1820) is on Patrick McGivney's stone — connecting the Malone NY McGivney family to the Creanew Nicholas who appears in 1828 Tithe AND 1857 Griffith's. If Patrick (b.~1782) is the Creanew patriarch, then the 1827 estate "Bernard" requires explanation. Two hypotheses: (1) Patrick used "Bernard" as his estate/official name ("Barney" = familiar form); (2) Bernard was Patrick's father (b.~1755-1765) who died ~1825-1827. Either way, the Malone NY cluster (Andrew, Patrick, Nicholas) and Creanew cluster (Andrew, Bryan, Michael, Nicholas, Thomas) appear to be the same family — some emigrated, some stayed.
S100.notes: Andrew McGivney (b.~1800 Cavan, d.Mar 23 1862 Malone NY) + wife Catherine (b.~1803 Cavan, d.Jun 9 1891). Both "Natives of Co. Caven Ireland" on tombstone. Their son Patrick Jr. (d.May 4 1862, age 23) died as Union soldier the same year as his father. A separate Barney McGivney (1846-1925) in Section 4 is likely Andrew's American-born son (Bernard Jr.). Patrick McGivney Sr. (b.~1782, d.Jan 26 1857) + Bridget Lynch (b.~1793, d.Jan 20 1879). Their daughter Bridget (1837-1911) = a sibling of Nicholas. Nicholas McGivney (b.~1820) appears in Creanew Crosserlough 1828 Tithe AND 1857 Griffith's AND in Malone NY on Patrick's stone — he likely emigrated from Creanew to Malone sometime after the Griffith's survey and died there 1905. Luke McGivney also in Section 5: "County Cavin, Ire. Parish Nock Bride" — from Knockbride Parish (different from Crosserlough); unrelated family. Bridget McGivney (d.Feb 5 1870, age 75) = Patrick Carlin's wife, "native of County Cavan" — may be related to the McGivney cluster or a different branch.

S98.person: 1901+1911 Irish Census — Kilnaleck DED (County Cavan) — McGivney and Gibney persistence; Drumcassidy McGivney-Reilly household 1911
S98.relatedPerson: Kilnaleck DED 1901 — 19 townlands including Drumcassidy, Drumhee (=Drumbee), Crosserlough, Kilnaleck village. Kilnaleck village 1901: 3 Gibney households (Houses 3,4,66) + 3 McGivney households (Houses 25,44,53) with House 25 containing McGivney+Reilly+Smyth. Drumcassidy 1901: McGivney at Houses 2,4,8; Reilly at Houses 1,5,10; total 11 households. Drumhee (=Drumbee) 1901: ZERO McGivney — Ulster Scots only (Godfrey, Hutchison, Wilson, Wilton, Heaslip, Smyth, Gray, Byers). Crosserlough townland 1901: 39 households, zero McGivney or Gibney (Reilly, Cusack, Lynch, Gaffney, Bird, Galligan dominant). Drumcassidy 1911: McGivney at Houses 3,4,5; House 4 has BOTH McGivney+Reilly (=McGivney-Reilly married couple or in-law). Kilnaleck village 1911: Gibney at House 10 (minimum; likely more in full list).
S98.clue: CRITICAL: McGivney-Reilly intermarriage confirmed across multiple generations (1828 Creanew + 1828 Drumcassidy + 1901 Kilnaleck House 25 + 1911 Drumcassidy House 4) — systematic McGivney-Reilly social bond spanning 83+ years. The Drumcassidy McGivney branch stayed in Ireland; the Drumbee/Creanew branches emigrated. Gibney continuous in Kilnaleck 1821→1901→1911. No McGivney in Drumbee by 1901 confirms Patrick Sr.'s family (John McGivney branch) fully emigrated by 1850.
S98.type: National Archives Ireland — 1901 and 1911 Irish Census (census.nationalarchives.ie)
S98.notes: Kilnaleck DED townlands (1901): Carrickacroy, Cookill part of Kilnaleck, Coolkill, Crosserlough, Cullow, Curviaghabreedin, Drumcassidy, Drumhee, Drumineslon, Drumrora, Drumscruddan, Finaway, Gallonregh, Kilnacrott, Kilnaleck, Mullycastle, Rockfield, Sallaghill, Toneylion. Notably ABSENT: Creanew/Creenow — the townland where Bryan McGivney lived in 1828 Tithe Books does NOT appear in the 1901 Kilnaleck DED list. By 1901 this tiny townland (only 8 households in 1828) may have been absorbed into adjacent Crosserlough or Kilnaleck townland designations. Drumhee = Drumbee (spelling variant in 1901 census): entirely Protestant/Ulster Scots by 1901, consistent with Patrick Sr.'s Catholic family having emigrated by 1850. Drumcassidy McGivney (1901 Houses 2,4,8): these are descendants of the Peter McGivney + Thomas McGivney first documented in 1828 Tithe Books — 73 years of continuity. House 4 (1911): McGivney+Reilly = continuation of the McGivney-Reilly intermarriage pattern established at least by 1828 (when James O'Reilly was in Creanew with Bryan McGivney and 3 Reilly households in Drumcassidy).

S97.type: National Archives Ireland — 1821 Census digital database
S97.notes: 51 total McGivney entries, County Cavan, 1821. All in Annagelliff Parish. Townlands: Corlurgan, Crumlin (two clusters), Crulany, Blake, Killagary, Edendeclare, Poles. S96.person: 1821 Census Kilnaleck village — Gibny family identification; BRIDGET GIBNY (b.~1801) confirmed as prime candidate for Patrick McGivney Sr.'s mother
S96.relatedPerson: CavanTownlands.ie Kilnaleck 1821 census: House 10 (John Gibny 27, servant labourer); House 11 (PATRICK GIBNY 48, Farmer & Mason + ANNE 43, wife/Flax Spinner + Michael 16 + Thomas 14 + Eliza 22 + BRIDGET 20, daughter); House 12 (Thomas Gibny 50, Farmer + Catherine 46 + Michael 16 + Catherine 12); House 13 (James Gibny 46, Farmer + Catherine 44 + Bernard 4 + Mary 8 + Catherine 6); House 14 (Bernard Gibny 45, Farmer + Catherine 31 + Patrick 4 + Mary 8 + Catherine 6 + Catherine 20 house servant). S96.clue: BRIDGET GIBNY, age 20 in 1821 (born ~1801), daughter of Patrick Gibny (House 11, Kilnaleck) is the prime identified candidate for Bridget Gibney — Patrick McGivney Sr.'s mother (S90). This identification now gives Bridget Gibney's parentage: daughter of Patrick Gibny (Farmer & Mason) and Anne (maiden name unknown) of Kilnaleck, Crosserlough Parish, County Cavan. Born ~1801.

S94.person: Tithe Applotment Books 1823–37 — Creanew (= Creenow) townland — BRYAN MCGIVNEY CONFIRMED + JAMES O'REILLY NEIGHBOR (SAME TOWNLAND) + Kilnaleck Gibney families + Drumkilly Reilly families

S93.relatedPerson: Free access via National Archives Ireland (titheapplotmentbooks.nationalarchives.ie). (2) DERRYLAHAN: Patt Reilly (listed as "Rielly Patt") visible in records 1–10 of 15; no McGivney in first 10 (remaining 5 may contain John McGivney). Other surnames: Magill (2), Bell (2), Smith (2), Byers (2) — predominantly Ulster Scots/Protestant pattern consistent with mixed townland. (3) Multiple Reilly families throughout Crosserlough: Drumvora (Bart Reilly, Jan 28 1828); Killytogher (Rev. Terr Reilly); Derrylea (Terce Reilly). (4) Creenow/Crenew: no results in Tithe Books (probably listed under variant spelling or not yet in records; 1827 estate record confirms Bryan McGivney in Creenow). S93.clue: CRITICAL CONTEXT: Drumcassidy had BOTH McGivney AND Reilly families living side by side in 1828 — 20 years before Griffith's Valuation shows McGivney households in same townland. The 3 Reilly households in Drumcassidy are almost certainly the source of Bryan McGivney's wife née Reilly (Peter Sr.'s mother). S93.notes: The Drumcassidy Tithe Books show: "Peter Magibny" + "Thos Mcgibney" (= Peter + Thomas McGivney) alongside "Peter Reilly" + "Patt Reilly" + "Jno Reilly" in the same townland. By Griffith's Valuation 1847–1864, Drumcassidy has Andrew, Mary, Thomas McGivney (continuity from 1828 Thomas). Drumcassidy is a short distance from Creenow — the two townlands are in the same compact parish area. Bryan McGivney (Creenow, 1827 land record + Griffith's) marrying a Reilly woman from Drumcassidy is the most parsimonious explanation for why his son Peter Sr. had a mother née Reilly. The Derrylahan Reilly (Patt Reilly) confirms Reilly families were present across multiple Crosserlough townlands adjacent to the McGivney cluster. McGivney, New Brunswick Canada (York County): community named after Irish McGivney settlers who established in the Nashwaak region; John McGivney b.1830 York County NB; James McGivney b.Sep 1857 Nashwaak — likely related to Andrew McGivney branch (Malone NY 1833) or a parallel Crosserlough emigrant to British North America ~1820s.
